last_updated: 2026-07-22 (round 2: 9 new Suno takes submitted to fix naptime/warm-acoustic and re-audition tender-strings, plus 3 extras)
loudness_target: I=-18 LUFS, TP=-2 dBTP (ffmpeg loudnorm, single-pass dynamic)
stability_gate: Round 2 gate (as specified for this pass): STEADY <6% deviant intervals, ACCEPTABLE 6-15%, REJECT >=15% deviant (deviant = inter-beat-interval BPM differing from median BPM by more than 6%). NOTE: round 1 slots (flagship-felt-piano, bright-week, morning-light, term-end, wildcard-warmth) were graded under a slightly looser round-1 gate (STEADY <=8%) and were not re-graded this round; their stored pct_deviant_intervals values are unchanged and still accurate, only the STEADY/ACCEPTABLE label boundary shifted for this round's new measurements. flagship-felt-piano (6.12%) would now read as ACCEPTABLE rather than STEADY under the round-2 gate if strictly reapplied -- flagged here for awareness, not changed.

warm-acoustic.tempo_interpretation: orig (direct detection) -- this take's raw beat tracker locked onto the musical pulse correctly on the first pass, no half/double correction needed
warm-acoustic.note: Round 1 was UNFILLED (Porchlight Album.mp3 rejected at 17.8%+ deviant under every tempo interpretation). Round 2 delivered two new takes both titled 'Porchlight Memory'; the (1) take is excellent (STEADY, 1.31% deviant, 83.35 vs target 84 -- 0.8% off) and is the clear winner over its sibling take (see takes log).

tender-strings.tempo_interpretation: half-time correction applied (raw beat tracker locked onto a doubled pulse at 143.55 BPM; decimating every 2nd beat from that track, offset=1, recovers a clean 73.83 BPM grid)
tender-strings.replacement_note: REPLACED round-1 holder 'Home Between Us.mp3' (measured 69.84 BPM, REJECT at 16.0% deviant, borderline-kept-and-flagged last round). Replacement criterion was 'meaningfully steadier (<12%)' -- Paper Boats Home.mp3 measures 1.41% deviant, an order of magnitude steadier than both the 16.0% reject and the 12% bar, and 73.83 BPM is closer to the 72 target than the old take's 69.84 BPM (2.5% off vs 3.0% off). Home Between Us.mp3 is retired from the slot; see takes log and superseded_round1_holders below.

naptime.tempo_interpretation: half-time correction applied (raw beat tracker locked onto a doubled pulse at 143.55 BPM; decimating every 2nd beat, offset=0, recovers a clean 69.84 BPM grid)
naptime.note: Round 1 was UNFILLED (Glass Snowglobe.mp3 rejected at 19.3% deviant, half-time interpretation was WORSE than raw). Round 2 delivered two new takes both titled 'Glass Moon Lullaby'; both are excellent (both STEADY once half-time corrected), the (1) take is marginally steadier and is the winner -- see takes log.

term-end.tempo_interpretation: half-time correction applied (raw beat tracker locked onto a faster pulse)

bonus-piano-keys.tempo_interpretation: half-time correction applied (raw beat tracker locked onto a doubled pulse at 143.55 BPM; decimating every 2nd beat, offset=1, recovers a clean 71.78 BPM grid)
bonus-piano-keys.note: Not a defined slot; measured per instructions as an 'extra'. Both 'Little Hands on Keys' takes are STEADY and land in-range (71.78 BPM, 60-100 window) so this qualifies as a bonus track. (1) take is marginally steadier than the plain take (0.75% vs 0.96% deviant) and wins -- see takes log.

bonus-lanterns.tempo_interpretation: half-time correction applied (raw beat tracker locked onto a doubled pulse at 143.55 BPM; decimating every 2nd beat, offset=1, recovers a clean 71.78 BPM grid)
bonus-lanterns.note: Single take, no pairing. STEADY and in-range (71.78 BPM, 60-100 window), so qualifies as a bonus track per instructions.

wildcard-warmth (Golden Afternoon prompt.loser_note: Same underlying tempo (78.3 BPM) but raw beat tracker locks onto double-time (161.5 BPM, artificially 'STEADY' at 1.9% because it's tracking 16th-note subdivisions, not the musical pulse). At the correct half-time interpretation it measures 78.30 BPM, 2.7% deviant -- still decent but slightly less steady than the (1) take, and its loudness profile (LRA 8.7 LU vs 3.7 LU) suggests more dynamic swells. (1) wins.

bright-week (Picnic Under Magnolia promp.loser_note: Same tempo (92.29 BPM), also STEADY, but 2.8% deviant vs winner's 0.51% -- winner is materially steadier. Loser is a fine backup if bright-week ever needs a B-side/variant.

naptime (Glass Moon Lullaby prompt, roun.loser_note: Both takes have the raw tracker lock onto a doubled pulse and both recover to clean, STEADY half-time grids once decimated: this take -> 66.26 BPM at 1.28% deviant (best offset), (1) take -> 69.84 BPM at 1.11% deviant (best offset). Both would have comfortably filled the slot; (1) wins narrowly on stability AND lands closer to the 68 BPM target (69.84 vs 66.26, 2.7% off vs 2.6% off -- essentially a wash on target proximity, so stability is the deciding factor). Loser is a strong backup/B-side candidate if naptime ever needs a variant.

warm-acoustic (Porchlight Memory prompt,.loser_note: Very different character between the two takes: the plain take's raw tracker locks onto a doubled pulse (161.5 BPM) and only reaches ACCEPTABLE-adjacent stability (4.29-5.43% deviant depending on decimation offset) after half-time correction. The (1) take's raw tracker found the true 83.35 BPM pulse directly on the first pass with no correction needed, and is far steadier (1.31% deviant). (1) wins clearly.

tender-strings (Paper Boats Home prompt,.loser_note: Both takes' raw trackers lock onto the same doubled pulse (143.55 BPM) and both recover to a clean 73.83 BPM half-time grid. This take is steadier at its best decimation offset (1.41% deviant, offset=1) vs the (1) take (2.68% deviant, offset=0). Both handily clear the <12% replacement bar and both would have been fine; the plain take wins on stability. Loser is a strong backup for tender-strings if a variant is ever needed.

bonus-piano-keys (Little Hands on Keys p.loser_note: Both takes are essentially identical in character: raw tracker locks onto a doubled 143.55 BPM pulse, both recover to a very clean 71.78 BPM half-time grid (iqr_bpm 0.0 on both). This take is marginally steadier (0.75% vs 0.96% deviant at best offsets). Both are excellent and interchangeable; loser is a strong backup/B-side.

unassigned_or_rejected.candidate_slot: no title hint; nearest targets are warm-acoustic (84) or naptime (68) by leftover-slot logic
unassigned_or_rejected.measured: Raw beat track locks onto 152 BPM (very steady, 1.1% deviant) but that's too fast for any slot (all targets 68-92). Half-time gives 76.0 BPM, which IS in-range and close to flagship-felt-piano/warm-acoustic territory, but stability degrades to 15.6% deviant -- just barely over the 15% REJECT line.
unassigned_or_rejected.verdict: AMBIGUOUS - left unassigned rather than guessed
unassigned_or_rejected.human_note: Two open problems stack here: (1) genuine half/double-time ambiguity (152 vs 76 -- both are 'clean' integer-ish readings so I can't disambiguate from tempo alone), and (2) the in-range interpretation is borderline-unstable (15.6% vs 15% cutoff). No title hint to break the tie either. I deliberately did NOT force this into naptime or warm-acoustic since both calls (which tempo is 'real', and whether the file is even steady enough) require a human ear. STILL UNRESOLVED as of round 2 -- no new take was submitted to address this file specifically, and it is now moot for naptime/warm-acoustic since both slots are filled, but it remains available as a fallback/spare if either slot's current take is ever rejected on listen.
